paid_impact_focus: all valid CodeHawks High, Medium, and Low impacts; internal Critical candidates map to CodeHawks High
target_scopes: Critical internal priority / CodeHawks High submission: catastrophic, readily exploitable loss or theft of most pool stake or bonus, protocol-wide insolvency, irreversible corruption of core settlement, or complete break of the pool's principal safety model by an unprivileged attacker.

High: direct or nearly direct loss, theft, permanent freezing, or unauthorized redirection of staker principal, bonus funds, attacker bounty, or recovery funds; repeat claims; major accounting insolvency; unprivileged control of resolution; or severe breakage of core pool settlement with high or medium likelihood.

Medium: indirect or conditional fund risk, meaningful loss under specific reachable conditions, material unfair bonus distribution, broken lifecycle/state transitions, persistent denial of important protocol functionality, stuck claims or withdrawals, scope/expiry/risk-window corruption, or factory/clone behavior that materially violates documented guarantees.

Low: concrete, reachable incorrect behavior or state handling with minimal financial impact, no direct funds at risk, and a real protocol/user consequence; exclude gas, QA, informational, style, speculative, and purely theoretical observations.
live_context_hint: source contest rather than a deployed bounty: pin contest URL, repository and commit, scope files, BattleChain assumptions, registry-state transitions, actors, trusted roles, ERC20 constraints, clone/factory topology, risk-window timestamps, k=2 bonus accounting, resolution snapshots, claim reserves, scope locks, and all known accepted design trade-offs
protocol_focus: A UUPS ConfidencePoolFactory deploys deterministic non-upgradeable ConfidencePool clones for active BattleChain Safe Harbor agreements. Pools custody ERC20 stake and optional bonus funds, lock a pool-local BattleChain account scope, observe registry lifecycle state live, seal risk windows, and resolve to SURVIVED, EXPIRED, or CORRUPTED. Survived/expired stakers receive principal plus a k=2 time-weighted bonus; corrupted pools pay a named good-faith attacker or sweep to the recovery address. Audit the complete CodeHawks-paid H/M/L surface, not only extraction.
attacker_profile: unprivileged staker, bonus contributor, prospective staker, pool creator acting only within documented sponsor powers, claimant, attacker-bounty claimant, arbitrary caller of permissionless poke/claim/sweep functions, malicious receiver contract, or caller exploiting a role-check bypass; never assume malicious factory owner, sponsor, moderator/DAO, registry owner, leaked key, or deliberately malicious trusted registry
core_invariants: only src/ConfidencePool.sol and src/ConfidencePoolFactory.sol are contest scope; vendored BattleChain interfaces, mocks, tests, scripts, and dependencies are context only
pool token balance must cover all live and snapshotted principal, bonus, bounty, and recovery liabilities, and no claimant may receive more than valid entitlement

eligibleStake, totalEligibleStake, totalBonus, time sums, snapshots, claimedBonus, corruptedReserve, bountyClaimed, and bountyEntitlement must reconcile across stake, withdraw, resolution, claim, sweep, donation, and re-flag paths

riskWindowStart and riskWindowEnd are one-way first-observation latches with timestamps safely bounded for every k=2 calculation; timing, rounding, clamping, and zero-score fallbacks cannot create an unfair or insolvent payout
scope is a valid pool-local subset when set and becomes immutable once the registry leaves NOT_DEPLOYED or NEW_DEPLOYMENT; expiry becomes immutable after the first successful stake
withdrawal stays available through all pre-attack states and becomes permanently unavailable after observed active risk; pause may block only stake and bonus inflows, never exits or settlement
outcomes and corrections follow registry-state gates; once claimsStarted records value movement, outcome finality cannot be reopened
good-faith CORRUPTED reserves the snapshotted pool for the named attacker for 180 days; bad-faith and expired bounty paths route remaining value exactly as documented
factory clone addresses, per-agreement indexes, initializer arguments, token allowlist checks, and UUPS authorization cannot be manipulated by an unprivileged caller
standard ERC20 behavior is assumed; fee-on-transfer and rebasing tokens are unsupported and must not be used as speculative attack premises
high_value_surfaces: stake, contributeBonus, withdraw, fee-on-transfer balance-difference handling, expiry boundaries, and registry deposit-state gates
flagOutcome correction rules, claimsStarted finality, outcome snapshots, goodFaith/attacker validation, and state-dependent SURVIVED/CORRUPTED decisions
claimSurvived, claimExpired, claimCorrupted, claimAttackerBounty, sweepUnclaimedCorrupted, and sweepUnclaimedBonus under partial claims, dust, donations, token balance deficits, and repeated calls
riskWindowStart/riskWindowEnd observation via all entrypoints and pokeRiskWindow, direct state skips, delayed observations, expiry caps, timestamp math, and uint32 limits
k=2 bonus numerator/denominator math, per-deposit time sums, user clamping, global snapshots, zero-score fallback, rounding, late entry, withdrawals, and multi-user ordering
setPoolScope validation and lock timing; agreement scope changes; duplicate, empty, zero, and out-of-agreement account lists; pool-local versus agreement-wide corruption

factory initialize/createPool, deterministic clone salts and indexes, initializer atomicity, implementation selection, stake-token allowlist, agreement ownership validation, and multiple pools per agreement
pause boundaries, expiry/recovery setters, two-step ownership, reentrancy and malicious receiver/token callbacks only within the supported standard-token model

cross-function and transaction-order interactions among registry transitions, staking, poking, withdrawal, moderator flagging, permissionless expiry resolution, claiming, re-flagging, sweeping, and post-resolution donations
impact_mapping: Critical internal -> CodeHawks High: catastrophic readily exploitable drain, protocol-wide insolvency, or complete irreversible break of core settlement.
High: principal/bonus/bounty theft or permanent freeze, repeated or excessive claims, unprivileged outcome control, major insolvency, or severe core-function failure with sufficient likelihood.

Medium: conditional or indirect fund loss, meaningful unfair distribution, persistent lifecycle or availability failure, stuck value, broken scope/expiry/finality guarantee, or material factory/clone malfunction.
Low: concrete reachable incorrect state or behavior with minimal impact and no direct funds at risk; must still have a real consequence and proof.

For compatibility with the current report schema, route value/accounting findings through fund_extraction and distribution/entitlement findings through reward_extraction, but do not reject other H/M/L findings described by target_scopes.
Submit CodeHawks High and Medium individually; aggregate Low findings as the current CodeHawks reporting guidance recommends.
known_rejection_memory: HARD REJECT any finding whose exploit premise is a malicious, sneaky, compromised, mistaken, or colluding factory owner, pool sponsor/owner, moderator/DAO, SafeHarborRegistry owner, attack-registry controller, proxy upgrader, recovery address, or leaked privileged key acting within documented authority.

Do not reject a real unprivileged role bypass, missing authorization, privilege escalation, or invariant failure triggered by an ordinary trusted action; the rejection applies to assuming trusted actors deliberately abuse their granted powers.

Reject all behavior expressly accepted in docs/DESIGN.md: active-risk states are legally attackable rather than breached; expiry during active risk resolves EXPIRED; UNDER_ATTACK deposits are allowed; first-claim finality closes re-flagging; local risk observation is decoupled from principal resolution; the no-risk-window CORRUPTED race is accepted; the 180-day auto-CORRUPTED fallback is scope-blind and staker-pessimistic; first-observed terminal timing residuals and the zero-score amount fallback are accepted; scope is pool-local and moderator-judged; the registry is a trusted singleton; corrupted sweep/bounty repeatability and donation recovery are intentional.

Reject fee-on-transfer, fee-on-sender, rebasing, malicious, callback-bearing, or otherwise non-standard ERC20 premises unless the issue remains exploitable with a standard ERC20; these token classes are explicitly unsupported.
Reject findings solely in interfaces, libraries, dependencies, mocks, scripts, tests, or BattleChain upstream contracts; they may be used only to prove impact in the two in-scope contracts.

Reject gas, QA, informational, style, comments, naming, best-practice-only, vague reentrancy, generic centralization, hypothetical future integration, and missing-event claims without a concrete paid H/M/L consequence.

Reject admin input/call-order mistakes, zero-address checks reachable only by trusted setters, ordinary market/MEV competition, user self-harm, accidental token transfers, unsupported integrations, and speculative liveness with no demonstrated protocol impact.

Reject duplicates of documented known behavior or identical root causes; require a reachable call sequence, affected invariant, impact/likelihood classification, and preferably a coded Foundry PoC before promotion.
CodeHawks has no separate Critical submission category for this contest; internally Critical candidates must be submitted as High if proven valid.
